entry.title: Re: "Help Needed: Identifying Standard CDS View for Sales...
entry.content:
Hello,

To find a CDS view that includes the version field for sales orders (field name: VSNMR_V), you may not find it in the basic I_SalesOrder CDS view. Instead, you should look within other CDS views that are specifically designed for value help or extended purposes. Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Sales Order

Here are steps you can take to address this requirement:

Using View Browser

  • You can use the ‘View Browser’ Fiori app to search for CDS views that might contain the field ‘VSNMR_V’. In the View Browser, you can search using the field name and check the available fields within each released view.

Check Associated CDS Views

There might be value help CDS views related to sales orders that include additional fields not present in the I_SalesOrder. You can explore views like:

  • I_SalesOrderStdVH

Creating a Custom CDS View

If no standard CDS view contains the field, you can create a custom CDS view:

  1. Custom CDS View App: Use the ‘Custom CDS Views’ app to create a new view. Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Sales Order (Value Help)
  2. Source Selection: Base your custom view on existing sales order views, but ensure to join or append the field ‘VSNMR_V’ from related tables/views.
  3. Field Inclusion: Explicitly include the ‘VSNMR_V’ field and any other necessary fields.
  4. Publishing and Testing: Publish the custom CDS view and test it to ensure it meets the data requirements.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

 

Using these steps, you can navigate through SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s CDS views and find or create the necessary data structure.

entry.title: Re: Setup External Pricing between Sales Cloud V2 and S/4...
entry.content:
Hello,

To set up External Pricing, you’ll need to ensure proper integration between the SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Here are the generalized steps you might consider taking:

  1. Ensure Connectivity:

    • Confirm that the SAP Cloud Connector is reachable and properly configured.

     

  2. Deploy iFlows in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI): SAP CLOUD FOR CUSTOMER INTEGRATION WITH S/4 HANA ON-PREMISE PART II

    • Login to CPI and navigate to the package named ‘SAP Cloud for Customer Integration with SAP S/4HANA’.
    • Identify appropriate iFlows and deploy, such as ’Pricing in Sales Quote Follow Up Scenario in SAP Business Suite’.

     

  3. Code List Mapping in Cloud for Customer:

    • Maintain code list mapping in the Cloud for Customer to ensure synchronization between systems.

     

  4. Set Up Communication Arrangements:

    • Create communication arrangements for various artifacts like Sales Quote Replication, Pricing scenarios, and Sales Order replication.

     

  5. CPI iFlow Configuration

    • Properly configure sender and receiver details for deployment.

     

  6. S/4HANA Configuration (If IDOC Adapter Type is Used):

    • For IDOC adapter types, maintain configurations in transactions WE20 & WE21.

     

  7. Execution Process:

    • Initiate the process from the Cloud for Customer system such as creating a Sales Quote, which triggers external pricing retrieval from S/4HANA and results in sales order creation.

     

By following these steps, you can facilitate accurate external pricing between Sales Cloud V2 and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, ensuring seamless integration and pricing consistency across both platforms.

For more detailed and situation-specific guidance, consulting the related SAP documentation might be advisable.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Set-up EDI connection - 2EL and 4A1
entry.content:
Hello,

When dealing with both scope items 2EL and 4A1, it is indeed possible to configure both, but it requires careful consideration regarding the communication arrangements. Let’s delve into the specifics:

Scope Item 2EL

  • 2EL focuses on the integration of sales with external buyers, 2780447 - Error message ‘No active outbound service could be found’ is raised when setting up Scope Item 2EL / Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0223 and you will need to set up the communication scenario SAP_COM_0223. This scenario is intended for customers who do not have a connection to SAP Business Network.

 

Scope Item 4A1

  • 4A1, on the other hand, is about automating order-to-invoice processes with SAP Business Network. Sales - Automation of Order-to-Invoice with SAP Business Network (4A1) The setup guide indicates that you need to deactivate SAP_COM_0223 in favor of setting up SAP_COM_0848. This scenario is intended for customers connected via SAP Business Network.
Can Both Scope Items Be Active?

Yes, it is possible to have both running simultaneously. You would set up SAP_COM_0223 for customers without SAP Business Network connections and SAP_COM_0848 for those with connections to SAP Business Network. However, you will need to manage business rules and configurations carefully to ensure that each customer’s transactions route through the correct communication scenario.

Considerations

  • Ensure distinct configurations: Differentiate the setups clearly to avoid conflicts.
  • Monitor integration flows: Regularly check both integrations to ensure data integrity and proper functionality.
  • Support Team Coordination: Engage with SAP support to resolve any integration issues timely.

Thus, while more complex, managing both setups is feasible and allows flexibility depending on your client’s customer needs.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu
